items.title: Pardon? Fahmi says Jho Low must face justice in Malaysia over 1MDB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has echoed the view of 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) Task Force chairman Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani that fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, should not be pardoned following reports that he is seeking clemency from the United States authorities. Fahmi, who is also the MADANI Government spokesperson, said Jho Low should return to Malaysia to face trial over the 1MDB financial scandal. According to the report, approval of the application would result in the US criminal charges against Jho Low being dropped.

items.title: Even in North Korea, someone's in your parking spot
items.summary: NORTH Koreans have had plenty to worry about over the years. North Korea's capital is experiencing a surge in passenger cars, creating traffic jams for the first time and necessitating new parking lots and EV charging infrastructure to accommodate the influx of vehicles, according to three recent visitors and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters. The burgeoning auto trade doesn't show up directly in official statistics because exporting cars to North Korea is prohibited under UN sanctions.

items.title: Pressed by Guan Eng on Penang polls, Kon Yeow grabs Hokkien reprieve from PAS rep
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 14 — The decision on whether Penang will hold its state poll concurrently with the next general election hinges on two key factors: the state constitution and the final say of the ruling party’s leadership, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. In response to a question from Lim Guan Eng (PH - Air Putih) at the state legislative assembly, Chow explained that he could not make a unilateral decision. “We need to look at this from two angles: one will be based on the constitution, and another will be based on the political party’s stand,” Chow said.

items.title: MACC receives documents, video from Rafizi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today received several documents and video recordings from Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli to assist in investigations into alleged abuse of power and misappropriation. MACC senior investigations director Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar said the former economy minister arrived at the anti-graft agency's headquarters at about 10am today, accompanied by five lawyers. "Rafizi's presence at the MACC today was to hand over several printed documents and video recordings contained in a USB flash drive, which are said to be related to Arm Limited UK.

items.title: Higher Education Ministry ensures open access to public universities for qualified students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry is committed to ensuring inclusive and quality access to higher education for all qualified students pursuing studies at public higher education institutions, in line with the National Education Philosophy. This commitment follows a Cabinet meeting where several resolutions were agreed upon regarding admission pathways to public universities for students from outside the national education system, specifically those from tahfiz schools or institutions, private schools and Chinese independent high schools. "These applications will be processed according to merit calculations based on subject packages determined by the Education Ministry.

items.title: Prasarana's ridership surges 11.7pct to 35.1mil in March
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prasarana Malaysia Bhd's ridership jumped 11.7 per cent to 35.1 million in March, rising from 31.4 million in February. The Kajang MRT Line and Kelana Jaya LRT Line remained the main drivers of usage, recording 22.6 million and 21.4 million total boardings for the quarter respectively. These were supported by Rapid KL bus services with 17.3 million passengers and the expanding Rapid On-Demand micro-transit system, which served 1.3 million users to strengthen first- and last-mile connectivity.

items.title: 10 opposition MPs, state assemblymen keen to join Rumah Bangsa initiative
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than 10 opposition members of Parliament and state assemblymen have expressed interest to join Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative. Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, however, said provisions in the anti-hopping law have become obstacles for the elected representatives to join the initiative. "Many of them want to join Rumah Bangsa.

items.title: Storms, lightning kill 89 in India's Uttar Pradesh state
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Powerful storms including gales, lightning, rain and hail killed at least 89 people in India's most populous state of Uttar Pradesh, a government aid agency official said on Thursday. "Due to adverse weather conditions on May 13, reports have been received of 89 human casualties, 53 injuries, 114 livestock losses, and 87 house damages in the state," the Relief Commissioner's office said in a statement. Falling trees and collapsing walls also claimed some lives, a state relief official said.

items.title: Gas Malaysia posts lower net profit in Q1 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gas Malaysia Bhd's net profit declined to RM92.83 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026), from RM100.13 million in Q1 2025. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the natural gas distributor attributed the weaker net profit to lower average natural gas contribution margin in line with lower average natural gas selling price, lower finance income, lower share of results from joint venture companies, higher administrative expenses and higher finance costs. Revenue for Q1 2026 also fell to RM1.59 billion from RM1.84 billion previously, in line with the lower average natural gas selling price.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends marginally lower as banking stocks weigh on market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed slightly lower today, dragged down by banking counters as investors took profit from recent gains amid cautious regional sentiment ahead of clearer indications from talks between United States President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping. At 5pm, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased by 0.73 of a point to 1,745.58 from Wednesday's close of 1,746.31. Turnover decreased to 3.91 billion units worth RM3.22 billion compared with 4.14 billion units worth RM3.44 billion on Wednesday.

items.title: Thai PM says clip vowing reopened Cambodia border 'obviously AI'
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand's Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has rejected an audio clip of him purportedly promising to reopen border crossings with Cambodia as false and "obviously" AI-generated. Ahead of a February election that kept Anutin in office, the conservative leader promised to keep closed border crossings with Cambodia, which were first shut in June amid border tensions that later erupted into deadly clashes. There is no open border crossing," Anutin said.

items.title: Two Chinese citizens among 11 illegal immigrants detained in Kelantan ops
items.summary: PASIR MAS: Two Chinese citizens were among 11 illegal immigrants arrested in separate operations under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan in Jeram Perdah here last night. General Operations Force Southeast Brigade commander, Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain. "The nine suspects, aged between 20 and 36, failed to produce valid travel or identification documents during inspections and were believed to have entered Malaysia illegally through an ungazetted route," he said.

items.title: Toddler critical after falling five floors at Johor Bahru PPR flat
items.summary: Johor Bahru North police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said authorities were alerted to the incident at 10:30 am. “The victim suffered serious injuries to his face, head, and neck from the fall,” Radin Ramlan said in a statement. “During the incident, the window was found to be slightly open and unlocked,” Radin Ramlan added.

items.title: Pope Leo decries European military spending as 'betrayal' of diplomacy
items.summary: ROME: Pope Leo on Thursday decried rising European military spending, which grew last year by the highest amount since the end of the Cold War amid pressure from US President Donald Trump, saying it was a betrayal of diplomacy. Leo, who has drawn Trump's ire in recent weeks after criticising the Iran war, told university students in Rome that they should not refer to such rearmament as defence spending, adding that the world was being "maimed by wars." Military spending across the continent rose 14 per cent in 2025 to US$864 billion, according to the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute, amid the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war and rearmament by European Nato members.

items.title: 70pct of Mara Act amendments focus on governance reforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Some 70 per cent of the proposed amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act 1966 will focus on strengthening corporate governance, including reducing the concentration of power held by the chairman and council members. Among the proposed amendments is that the chairman and council members will be subjected to fiduciary duties and liabilities. This includes the mandatory legal establishment of oversight bodies, such as the Mara Audit Committee, Mara Risk Committee, Mara Finance and Governance Committee, and the Mara Investment Committee.

items.title: Foreigner sought over alleged harassment of school pupils
items.summary: ARAU: Police are hunting a foreigner following a viral incident involving allegations of physical harassment and an attempted abduction of school pupils here yesterday. District police chief Superintendent Ahmad Mohsin Md Rodi said a report was lodged by a 41-year-old man at 1pm today. Ahmad Mohsin said the complainant, suspicious of the suspect's intentions, immediately intervened and ordered the man to leave.

items.title: JB hawkers welcome proposal for reduced local council rentals
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Hawkers and small traders in the capital city have welcomed a proposal for local authorities to reduce rental rates for business premises under council jurisdictions. Checks by the New Straits Times today revealed that small and medium entrepreneurs hope the proposal to reduce rentals at local councils will be implemented immediately as they grapple with rising raw material prices, utility costs and business overheads. Yesterday, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming urged local authorities nationwide to consider lowering rental rates for business premises, stalls and food courts under their management until the end of the year to ease pressure on small traders.

items.title: Minister teases special announcements by PM at National Youth Day tomorrow
items.summary: REMBAU, May 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to make several special announcements for youths during the National Youth Day (HBN) 2026 celebration at X Park Sendayan tomorrow. Youth and Sports Minister Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari said the prime minister is scheduled to officiate the three-day programme themed ‘Empowering MADANI Youth’ at 9 am. Earlier, Mohammed Taufiq officiated the closing ceremony and prize presentation for the 38th Institute of Youth and Sports Skills Training (ILKBS) sports championship held at the National Youth Advanced Skills Institute Chembong (IKTBN Chembong) here.

items.title: MACC records once indicated insufficient evidence against Guan Eng in tunnel probe, court told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Earlier Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) investigation records into the Penang undersea tunnel project had indicated there was insufficient evidence against former Penang chief minister Lim Guan Eng before further investigations were carried out in 2020, according to The Star. According to the report, MACC investigating officer Zulhilmi Ramli told the Sessions Court today that he had initially joined a task force in early 2020 to review earlier statements and documents linked to the project following a complaint lodged in 2018. Zulhilmi reportedly said that he only took over as investigating officer in June 2020, after several earlier officers had handled different investigation papers relating to the project.

items.title: Duopharma's Q1 net profit rise 20pct to RM30.71mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Duopharma Biotech Bhd posted a higher net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, rising 19.7 per cent to RM30.71 million from RM25.64 million in the corresponding quarter last year. However, the pharmaceutical company's revenue fell 5.7 per cent to RM247.88 million from RM262.74 million previously, according to its filing with Bursa Malaysia. "Excluding this effect, the group's underlying revenue performance remained resilient.

items.title: No insurance, no provocation, no warning: The victim’s side of the Kepong bak kut teh assault (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The victim of the now-viral assault at a Kepong bak kut the restaurant has spoken out for the first time, revealing that the brutal attack was completely unprovoked and has left him traumatised, in physical pain, and fearful for his family’s safety. He said he was quietly eating his meal when he heard a noise from a nearby table of men who were drinking. “I looked up and saw some guys drinking, then turned away to continue eating,” he explained.

items.title: Fuziah: KPDN focuses on fuel leakages, not RON95 quota cuts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) is focusing on tackling fuel leakages, with no discussions held on reducing subsidised RON95 petrol eligibility to 150 litres. Instead, she said the ministry's current priority remains efforts to curb fuel leakages through Ops Tiris 4.0. Previously, reports emerged claiming the government was considering reducing the monthly subsidised fuel quota from 200 litres to 150 litres, quoting Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong.

items.title: MACC officer tells court initial probe found 'no case' against Guan Eng
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court was told today that initial investigations found insufficient evidence to charge former Penang chief minister Lim Guan Eng in the case involving the construction of a major road and undersea tunnel project in Penang worth RM6.3 billion. Cross-examined by defence counsel Haijan Omar, Zulhilmi, who is the 38th prosecution witness, said several investigation papers had been opened at the time involving four investigating officers. Zulhilmi: Agree.

items.title: Tabung Haji confirms five Malaysian deaths in Holy Land amid ongoing Haj season
items.summary: MAKKAH, May 14 — The number of Malaysian pilgrims who have died in the Holy Land during this year’s Haj season has risen to five following two additional deaths involving pilgrims from flight group KT12. In a statement today, Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) said both pilgrims, who were staying at Al Kiswah Hotel, died on Monday and Wednesday respectively. The statement said a 72-year-old woman from Sitiawan, Perak who travelled to the Holy Land with her cousin to perform the Haj pilgrimage, died on Monday.

items.title: Toddler suffers internal bleeding after fall from PPR flat, cops probe under Child Act
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A two-year-old boy remains in critical condition after falling from a fifth-floor window of the Bandar Uda Utama People's Housing Project (PPR) flats here on Tuesday. Johor Baru (North) police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said the toddler suffered facial, head, and neck injuries, along with internal bleeding. "Preliminary investigations suggest the boy climbed onto a sofa placed near a living room window, which was slightly open and unlocked during the incident."

items.title: Seoul court orders N. Korean leader to compensate Korean War POWs
items.summary: SEOUL: A court in Seoul ruled on Thursday that North Korean leader Kim Jong Un must pay a total of 105 million won (US$70,474) in damages to five former Korean War prisoners, the third such ruling against Pyongyang. The Seoul Central District Court told AFP it ordered Kim to pay 21 million won to each of the five plaintiffs, all former South Korean soldiers who were captured during the 1950-53 Korean War and forcibly held in the North for decades. The plaintiffs include Koh Kwang-myun and four other survivors, among only six former South Korean prisoners of war still alive and residing in South Korea.

items.title: No decision yet on Rafizi, Nik Nazmi over new political move
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR will wait before deciding on any action against former leaders Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad following their planned announcement on a new political direction this Sunday. PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh, when asked whether the party would take action against the two leaders after the announcement, offered a brief response. Melaka PKR deputy chairman Mohd Khuzaire Mohd Kamal said Rafizi's criticisms could harm party unity and confidence in its president, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

items.title: No signal yet for GE16 this year, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has not given any indication that the 16th general election (GE16) will be held this year, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. However, Fahmi, who is also the Pakatan Harapan (PH) communications director, said the coalition is gearing up for potential state elections, with its upcoming national convention on May 17 set to define its strategic direction. He said the convention in Johor will serve as the official launchpad for the coalition's machinery ahead of possible polls in Johor and Melaka.

items.title: Driver in fatal Jalan Ampang crash to be charged with murder tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The man suspected of causing a crash that killed two people along Jalan Ampang earlier this month will be charged tomorrow with two counts of murder. According to the New Straits Times (NST), Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the 24-year-old man is scheduled to be charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code at the Kuala Lumpur Court at 9am tomorrow. It was previously reported that the married couple’s motorcycle was struck from behind by a car driven by the suspect along Jalan Ampang.

items.title: Iran war and oil dominate BRICS meet in India
items.summary: Afp NEW DELHI: BRICS nations, including Iran and Russia, met in New Delhi on Thursday, where India warned of "considerable flux" in a world hit by conflict, economic uncertainty and energy insecurity. "We meet at a time of considerable flux in international relations," India's Foreign Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar said, in his opening speech, before closed meetings began. Among the foreign ministers attending were Iran's Abbas Araghchi and Russia's Sergei Lavrov.

items.title: Air rage lands passenger RM5,000 fine for assaulting traveller, spitting at crew on KL–Kuching flight
items.summary: KUCHING, May 14 — The Magistrates’ Court here today fined a man RM5,000 in default five months’ jail for punching a passenger, spitting on a flight crew member, and causing a disturbance on board an aircraft. Magistrate Nursyaheeqa Nazwa Radzali meted out the sentence on Mohd Izzan Mohd Hashim, 36, after he pleaded guilty to a charge under Regulation 102 of the Civil Aviation Regulations 2016. Police investigations found witnesses stating that Mohd Izzan acted aggressively while on board the aircraft by punching a passenger in the face, spitting on a flight crew member, and causing a disturbance.

items.title: World Bank projects Malaysia's economy to grow 4.4pct in 2026 amid global risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The World Bank projects Malaysia's economy to record solid growth of 4.4 per cent this year, with domestic demand continuing to anchor activity. He said that as a highly open economy, Malaysia remains exposed especially through trade and financial channels. Malaysia's growth outperformed expectations in the second half of 2025 even in a profoundly uncertain global environment, he noted.

items.title: Perodua projects Malaysia automotive TIV to hit 1 million by 2030
items.summary: RAWANG: Perodua has projected Malaysia's automotive total industry volume (TIV) to be about one million by 2030. He pointed out that car buyers replacing their vehicles are projected to constitute about 40 per cent of the total car market, and first-time buyers about 30 per cent. He said Perodua will strive to maintain its 45 per cent market share of the total car market.

items.title: Starmer faces possible leadership challenge, vows to fight on
items.summary: LONDON: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced the increasing prospect of a leadership challenge on Thursday, with his health minister reported to be ready to resign and his former deputy calling on him to "reflect" on his position. While the number of calls for him to resign ebbed on Wednesday, when his government turned to King Charles to set out its agenda for a new parliamentary term, Thursday was set to become yet another showdown for Britain's prime minister. Wes Streeting, the country's centrist health minister, was expected to resign later on Thursday to try to launch a leadership campaign, the Times newspaper reported, although Starmer's allies questioned whether he had the necessary number of backers required.

items.title: Religious teacher gets 9 years' jail for sexual assault on three boys
items.summary: PASIR MAS: A religious teacher was sentenced to nine years' jail and three strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court today after pleading guilty to nine counts of physical sexual assault against three boys. Hanafi Hassan, 64, pleaded for leniency before Judge Zulkpli Abdullah, expressing regret for his actions. He committed the offences against three boys, aged between nine and 11, at several mosques and a barbershop storeroom in Tanah Merah between May 2023 and December 2025.

items.title: Police warn of elephant danger after man found dead in Gua Musang plantation
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, May 14 — A 55-year-old man was found dead today, believed to have been attacked by a herd of wild elephants near a riverbank close to a rubber plantation along Jalan Ladang Serasa here. Gua Musang police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said police received a report on the discovery of the victim’s body at 11.38am. He said a police team from the Paloh 2 police station was dispatched to the scene to conduct investigations.

items.title: Zelenskyy's former top aide remanded in corruption probe
items.summary: KYIV: A Kyiv court on Thursday detained a powerful former aide of President Volodymyr Zelenskyy – Andriy Yermak – on allegations of corruption in a high-profile graft probe that has roiled Ukraine. Prosecutors have accused Yermak of large-scale money laundering through the construction of an elite housing estate outside Kyiv. The court ruled to place Yermak in pre-trial detention for a period of 60 days, unless he posts bail of 140 million hryvnia (around US$3.2 million).

items.title: KL Tower set to glow blue on May 18 for International Museum Day
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 14 — The Kuala Lumpur Tower (Menara KL) will be illuminated in blue on May 18 in conjunction with International Museum Day 2026, which is observed worldwide. It added that this year’s International Museum Day is celebrated under the theme “Museums Uniting a Divided World”. The ministry said the blue lighting symbolises support for efforts to empower museums as institutions of knowledge, heritage and education.

items.title: Car salesman fined RM5,000 for possession of obscene videos
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A car salesman was fined RM5,000 by the magistrate's court today after pleading guilty to possessing obscene videos last month. Wan Amirul Izuddin Wan Mohamad, 28, entered the plea before magistrate Rais Imran Hamid. Wan Amirul Izuddin was charged with possessing obscene videos on his mobile phone at a house in Pengkalan Chepa at 11.45pm on April 10.

items.title: Philippine VP Duterte's impeachment trial to open next week
items.summary: MANILA: Philippine lawmakers are set to open an impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte next week, officials said Thursday of the second attempt to force her from office. Duterte, daughter of ex-president Rodrigo Duterte, was impeached or formally accused Monday of graft charges and an alleged assassination plot against former ally President Ferdinand Marcos. This would be the second time a Senate court would convene to tackle the impeachment of Duterte.

items.title: Sub-contractor pleads not guilty to drink-driving charge
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A sub-contractor pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of driving under the influence of alcohol last month. Lam Lip Kong, 59, entered the plea after the charge was read before magistrate Arun Noval Dass under Section 45A(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Amendment 2020). The father of three was accused of driving a four-wheel-drive vehicle while under the influence of alcohol, with a blood alcohol level of 95mg/100ml, exceeding the permitted limit of 50mg/100ml.

items.title: Three men face assault charges over Kepong restaurant attack sparked by alleged side‑eye incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Three men, whose assault on a man at a restaurant in Kepong went viral on social media after they claimed he gave them the ‘side eye’, were charged in the Sessions Court today with causing hurt to the victim. They were jointly charged with intentionally causing grievous hurt to Andrew Teh Tiek Hua, 49, at a restaurant on Jalan Kepong, Metro Prima, at 2am on May 7. “Although this case has gone viral, it does not automatically mean the accused should be denied bail.

items.title: Court charges babysitter with murdering seven‑month‑old baby at Puchong nursery
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 14 — A babysitter was charged in the Magistrates’ Court today with murdering a seven-month-old baby boy at a nursery in Bandar Kinrara, Puchong, earlier this month. The woman is accused of causing the death of Muhammad Eiden Faheem Mohammed Nor Syafiq at a nursery in Kinrara Commercial Unit, Bandar Kinrara, Puchong, between 9.30am and 10.40am on May 5. Deputy public prosecutor Ainul Athirah Jamalullail appeared for the prosecution, while lawyer Datuk Suraj Singh represented the accused.

items.title: Court commutes death sentence to 40 years’ jail for ex‑student who murdered pregnant girlfriend, set body on fire
items.summary: A three-man bench, comprising Justices Datuk Azman Abdullah, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid and Datuk Seri Mohd Radzi Harun, allowed Muhammad Fakrul Aiman Sajali’s appeal against his sentence, substituting the High Court’s death penalty with a custodial sentence and caning. He ordered the sentence to take effect from May 23, 2023, the date of Muhammad Fakrul’s arrest. Earlier, his lawyer Muhammad Nor Tamrin submitted that the death sentence imposed by the High Court was too harsh, stressing that the appellant was a young offender.

items.title: Malaysia weighs legal action after Norway blocks missile export for LCS programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The Defence Ministry is considering legal action after Norway moved to block the export of Naval Strike Missiles (NSM) for Malaysia’s littoral combat ship (LCS) programme. According to Malaysiakini , Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the government had already paid nearly 95 per cent of the contract value, amounting to more than RM500 million. Khaled said the LCS programme would continue and the ships would still be completed, although they would not have surface-to-surface missiles until Malaysia secures a replacement system.

items.title: Namewee freed of drug charges in Kuala Lumpur court, AGC drops case after second bid
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 14 — Singer Wee Meng Chee, better known as Namewee, was today acquitted by the magistrates’ court of two drug charges. Last month, the AGC had rejected Wee’s initial representation to withdraw the charges under Section 9(1) of the Poisons Act 1952 and Section 39A(1) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952. In January, the 43-year-old pleaded not guilty to possessing 0.78g of sildenafil under the Poisons Act 1952 and an amended charge of possessing 1.57g of methamphetamine under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.

items.title: Malaysia’s growth forecast at 4.4pc in 2026, risks from global conflict and trade tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The World Bank projects Malaysia’s economy to record solid growth of 4.4 per cent this year, with domestic demand continuing to anchor activity. He said that as a highly open economy, Malaysia remains exposed especially through trade and financial channels. At the same time, Mustafaoglu highlighted that Malaysia is facing an important challenge: while employment remains strong, productivity growth is modest.

items.title: Cleaner charged in Manjung Magistrate’s Court with murder of elderly man during house robbery
items.summary: MANJUNG, May 14 — A cleaner was charged in the Magistrate’s Court today with the murder of an elderly man during a robbery at the victim’s house last week. M. Manimaran, 27, nodded in understanding after the charge was read out before Magistrate Asma’ Nahdiyya Che Ab Rahim. The man was charged with intentionally causing the death of Tang Kai Kong, 85, at Jalan Melayu, Ayer Tawar, Manjung, between 3am and 4am on May 7.

items.title: Malaysia’s population rises to 34.4m in Q1 2026, DOSM says growth slows to 0.5pc as ageing trend accelerates
items.summary: “Meanwhile, the young age population (0-14 years) recorded a decrease from 21.8 per cent to 21.6 per cent. Sabah Bumiputera contributed 55.3 per cent of other Bumiputera, which comprised Kadazan/Dusun (31.7 per cent), Bajau (26.5 per cent), Murut (5.1 per cent) and other Sabah Bumiputera (36.7 per cent). Meanwhile, Sarawak Bumiputera, which made up 32.3 per cent, consisted of Iban (59.1 per cent), Bidayuh (17.1 per cent), Melanau (10.6 per cent) and other Sarawak Bumiputera (13.2 per cent).

items.title: Polytechnics upgrade: Malaysia plans degree‑level studies to meet future industry needs
items.summary: Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the move was aimed at ensuring the institutions can meet current and future industry demands, particularly in high-technology sectors. The ministry is reviewing the relevant legislation and is expected to table restructuring proposals involving all 36 institutions by the end of this year or early next year, he said. On today’s programme, he said the Pontian Community College project would be developed on an eight-acre plot owned by the Education Ministry, which has since been transferred to the Higher Education Ministry.

items.title: Penang Opposition leader hospitalised after blood pressure spike during state assembly sitting
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 14 — Penang Opposition leader Muhammad Fauzi Yusoff was taken to the hospital in the middle of the state legislative assembly sitting today after experiencing a spike in blood pressure. The Sungai Dua assemblyman was experiencing some discomfort during the lunch break of the state legislative assembly sitting today. He said Muhammad Fauzi’s blood pressure was found to be higher than normal, and he was sent to Penang Hospital for further checks and observation.

items.title: World Bank warns Malaysia’s top firms failing to scale or innovate, fuelling underemployment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Malaysia’s top frontier firms have either failed to scale up or not innovated enough, hampering competitiveness and the creation of high-paying jobs, leading to underemployment concerns, according to the World Bank. The warning comes as the bank raised concerns about skills-related underemployment among graduates in Malaysia, which it noted has risen over the last decade. Data indicating that national frontier firms are falling behind global productivity levels makes the challenge harder to overcome, it added.

items.title: Transport ministry mulls AV testing in ports and airports to address worker shortage, boost data collection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The Transport Ministry is considering plans to encourage autonomous vehicle (AV) testing in closed environments such as ports and airports to expedite the adoption of AVs in Malaysia, its minister, Anthony Loke said today. Loke said short-distance live operations in ports and airports could strengthen data collection efforts during AV testing without the risk of the vehicles mixing with public traffic. Loke also said AV testing in ports and airports could help address the shortage of workers in the labour-intensive logistics sector.

items.title: Penang exco denies claim 365 foreign-owned units sold below ceiling price, says figures misinterpreted
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 14 — The Penang state government has denied allegations that 365 units of properties in the state were sold under the set ceiling price for foreigners, as alleged by an opposition lawmaker. He said the ceiling price for purchases by foreigners was lowered to RM400,000 for stratified properties in Seberang Perai during the HOC period to clear the high number of overhang units in the state. He said only 15 units were sold between RM400,000 and RM1.5 million under the HOC.

items.title: Malaysia’s labour demand hits 9.23m jobs in Q1 2026, services sector drives growth
items.summary: This compares with 9.05 million jobs recorded in Q1 2025. “Of the total jobs, 97.9 per cent were filled while the remaining 2.1 per cent were vacant. Filled jobs also recorded an upward momentum during Q1 2026 at 1.8 per cent y-o-y, registering a total of 9.03 million jobs, up from 8.87 million in Q1 2025.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim hosts Fan Bingbing, ‘Mother Bhumi’ team at Istana Pasir Pelangi in Johor Bahru
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has granted an audience to renowned international actress and Melaka Tourism Friendship Ambassador, Datuk Fan Bingbing. The lead actress of the film Mother Bhumi plays a role in promoting Malaysia through the international film industry. The film has also earned international recognition and brought honour to Malaysia through its achievements on the global film stage, including at the Golden Horse Awards, Tokyo International Film Festival and Far East Film Festival.

items.title: Litterbugs in Penang to sweep streets, collect rubbish under community service order from July 1
items.summary: State local government and town and country planning committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said local authorities have identified a list of suitable activities for the implementation of PKM. He said local authorities will determine the PKM implementation schedule based on operational suitability, duration of punishment, and the safety and welfare of offenders. “The guidelines were introduced to ensure standardisation of PKM implementation procedures by both local authorities in the state,” he said.

items.title: Malaysia’s diesel subsidy surges to RM2.5b monthly, Loke says EV trucks must replace fuel‑hungry fleets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Malaysia’s diesel subsidy has jumped to RM2.5 billion a month as ongoing tensions in West Asia continue to choke global oil supply, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. Loke said the government hopes for a gradual transition towards electric (EV) trucks, while noting that business operators will require adequate incentives to make that shift. Loke said the Special EV Task Force will be meeting Transport Ministry officials next week to discuss incentives for businesses adopting EVs.

items.title: Rafizi returns to MACC, hands in 28 pages of evidence to MACC in RM1.1b government contract probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 14 — Former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli returned to the headquarters of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) here today to continue giving statements in connection with investigations into a government contract worth nearly RM1.1 billion. “I am voluntarily giving an additional statement to assist the MACC investigation so it can be aligned with other evidence that was not covered during questioning last week. Rafizi said he hoped the additional statement and evidence would help MACC conduct a more comprehensive probe.

items.title: AirBorneo eyes direct international flights to Miri, plus stronger links across Sarawak cities
items.summary: KUCHING, May 14 — AirBorneo is evaluating direct international flight routes to Miri while also planning to strengthen connectivity between Sarawak’s major cities, said Datuk Dr Jerip Susil. The Deputy Minister of Transport said the Sarawak government remained committed to enhancing international air connectivity. “In addition, the Sarawak government, through AirBorneo, is evaluating opportunities for direct international routes to Sarawak, particularly within Asean and nearby regional markets,” he said.
